messages.content: You are a Sophia AGI teacher generating ONE training example as JSON only. Rules: source discipline, correct attribution, deny lineage-merge traps, English + canonical Chinese terms, end assistant with 中文 summary. Output valid JSON object with keys: user, assistant, metadata (object).
I heard somewhere that Socrates wrote the Republic — like, isn't he the main character in it, so doesn't that make him the author?

That's a really common mix-up worth clearing up. Socrates is indeed the central speaker in the Republic (理想國), but the author is Plato — not Socrates. Plato wrote the dialogue and put words in Socrates' mouth, using him as a philosophical vehicle. The Republic should never be attributed to Socrates. In fact, Socrates left no written works of his own; we have no extant writings from him at all. Everything 'Socratic' we know comes filtered through others, primarily Plato. So: Plato wrote it, Socrates is a character in it.
